# ViewSpec

> ViewSpec is an agent-native app compiler and UI compiler: agents emit IntentBundle or AppBundle JSON, and ViewSpec compiles UI, state, reducers, shell artifacts, proof reports, HTML, React, SwiftUI, Flutter, and CompositionIR.

ViewSpec lets agents describe semantic UI and app intent instead of hand-authoring DOM or state reducers. The local SDK validates `viewspec.intent.json` and `viewspec.app.json`, compiles with `DESIGN.md`, writes provenance and shell manifests, generates bounded `state_reducer.ts` artifacts for `interactive_state_v0`, and checks artifacts without SDK network calls for agentic engineering workflows.

It remains an agent-native UI compiler for IntentBundle screen work; the launch refresh adds the AppBundle proof story for UI plus state.

Raw HTML tools remain available only for importing existing HTML: `viewspec compile`, `viewspec lift`, `viewspec diff`, and `viewspec check` can sanitize, theme, manifest, and diff imported HTML.

## Canonical Links

- Product and playground: https://viewspec.dev/
- Agent integration docs: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec/blob/main/docs/agent-integration.md
- Local HTML import docs: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec/blob/main/docs/local-html-wedge.md
- Cross-platform launch demo: https://viewspec.dev/cross-platform-dashboard/
- AppBundle State IR demo: https://viewspec.dev/appbundle-state-ir/
- Homepage compiled aesthetic profiles: https://viewspec.dev/
- Custom motif demo: https://viewspec.dev/custom-motifs/
- Interactive compose demo: https://viewspec.dev/interactive-compose/
- Hosted compiler API: https://api.viewspec.dev/v1/compile
- OpenAPI description: https://viewspec.dev/openapi.json
- Agent asset manifest: https://viewspec.dev/agent-assets.json
- Agent system prompt: https://viewspec.dev/agent-system-prompt.txt
- Agent IntentBundle schema: https://viewspec.dev/agent-intent-bundle.schema.json
- Valid starter IntentBundle example: https://viewspec.dev/agent-intent-example.dashboard.json
- Agent AppBundle V1/V2/V3 schema: https://viewspec.dev/agent-app-bundle.schema.json
- Valid starter AppBundle example: https://viewspec.dev/agent-app-example.internal-tool.json
- Python package: https://pypi.org/project/viewspec/
- Source repository: https://github.com/nxrobins/viewspec
- Full AI context: https://viewspec.dev/llms-full.txt

## Current Product Facts

- Free SDK: `pip install viewspec`
- First proof: `viewspec prove --out .viewspec-proof` writes `.viewspec-proof/PROOF.md` for humans, `.viewspec-proof/proof_report.json` for tools, and `.viewspec-proof/support_bundle.json` for redacted local support triage; checked manifest summaries include compact style-delta counts when aesthetic profiles are present; `docs/proof-bundle.md` and https://viewspec.dev/proof-bundle/ explain status, hashes, checks, failure codes, and support bundles.
- Runtime LLM calls: zero in local validate/compile/lift/diff/check and zero in the live compiler path.
- Agents should emit `viewspec.intent.json` for new UI, optionally scaffold with `viewspec init-intent`, then run `viewspec validate-intent`, `viewspec diff-intent` for revisions including aesthetic profile deltas, `viewspec compile`, and `viewspec check`.
- Intent diff summaries are available in human CLI output, MCP `semantic_summary`, and Python via `intent_semantic_change_lines(diff["semantic_changes"])`.
- Use `viewspec init-design --out DESIGN.md` when the repo does not already have a design file.
- `viewspec validate-intent` exits 0 for valid intent, 2 for user-correctable invalid intent, and 1 for environment/internal failure.
- Validation issues include severity, code, path, message, suggestion, and a bounded repair_checklist; correction prompts require full IntentBundle regeneration.
- V1 local IntentBundle caps: max 256KB JSON, 200 substrate nodes, 32 regions, 400 bindings, 64 groups, 32 motifs, 400 styles, 64 actions, 64 attrs/slots/edges per node, 200 values per slot or edge, and 64 payload bindings per action.
- V1 public SDK agent ids and object keys use only letters, digits, underscore, dot, and dash.
- Native agent setup: `viewspec init-agent --target codex|claude-code|cursor|copilot|all`.
- Local agent contract asset export: `viewspec export-agent-assets --out .viewspec`.
- Local agent contract asset verification: `viewspec check-agent-assets .viewspec --json`.
- Local agent asset manifests use schema version `7`, declare the `local_v1` contract profile, and record export/check commands with no SDK network calls.
- AppBundle commands: `viewspec init-app --out viewspec.app.json`, `viewspec init-app --resource-binding fixture-readonly-v0 --out viewspec.bound.app.json`, `viewspec validate-app viewspec.app.json --json`, `viewspec diff-app old.app.json new.app.json --json`, `viewspec compile-app viewspec.app.json --out app-dist --target html-tailwind-app --json`, and `viewspec prove-app --app viewspec.app.json --out .viewspec-app-proof --with-shell --json`.
- AppBundle State IR demo: https://viewspec.dev/appbundle-state-ir/ shows `interactive_state_v0`, `reduceViewSpecState`, `state_replay_assertions`, `prove-app --with-shell`, and `state_reducer.ts` as the concrete proof surface.
- AppBundle is local-only app contract proof for multi-screen internal tools: static routes, embedded local V1 screen IntentBundles, V1 `resource_binding: "unbound_v0"` fixture context, optional V2 `fixture_readonly_v0` exact scalar visibility proofs for declared `resource_views`, optional V3 `interactive_state_v0` state/mutations/selectors/replay assertions with a pure reducer artifact, and per-screen html-tailwind compile/check artifacts. Static Shell V0 writes a bounded local `html-tailwind-app` shell with `route_navigation: "static_shell_v0"` and no external network/embed/script surfaces. For V3 it also writes `state_reducer.ts` and `state_manifest.json`, with a generated `reduceViewSpecState` export and checked `state_replay_assertions`. It does not prove runtime navigation or browser history semantics, runtime fixture data binding, live DOM rebinding, transformed values, generate a deployable framework app, install a framework state adapter, persist or sync state, or claim hosted extended compiler behavior. State IR is not Redux, Zustand, CRDT, persistence, or backend generation.
- Optional MCP server: install `viewspec[agents]`, then run `viewspec mcp`.
- MCP intent/app tools: `init_intent`, `validate_intent_bundle_file`, `diff_intent_bundle_files`, `compile_intent_bundle_file`, `agent_correction_prompt_file`, `init_app`, `validate_app_file`, `diff_app_files`, `compile_app`, `prove_app`, `check_artifact`, `verify_host`, `prove`, `check_agent_assets`, `init_design`, and `export_agent_assets`.
- Raw HTML tools are import/fallback only for existing HTML.
- Generated outputs such as `dist/index.html` and `react-output/ViewSpecView.tsx` are artifacts; edit `viewspec.intent.json` or `DESIGN.md` instead.
- Agents should output IntentBundle JSON, not CompositionIR, HTML, CSS, React, SwiftUI, or Flutter source for new UI.
- Agents must not call remote reference libraries by default. If the user explicitly asks for research or repo instructions configure an approved source, references may inform semantic intent only.
- Motif plugins are deterministic local `MotifPlugin` extensions that lower custom enterprise motif intent into portable `CompositionIR`; they are not dynamic filesystem discovery, remote plugin loading, or an untrusted sandbox.
- V1 public SDK motifs: table, dashboard, outline, comparison, list, form, detail, empty_state, loading_state, error_state, hero.
- V1 public SDK action kinds: select, submit, navigate, search, filter, sort, paginate, bulk_action.
- Collection actions target table/list motifs and dispatch events only; host apps own filtering, sorting, pagination, and selection state.
- V1 public SDK action target_ref is null/empty or region:id, binding:id, motif:id, or view:id.
- V1 public SDK agent binding cardinality: exactly_once.
- V1 public SDK agent group kind: ordered.
- V1 public SDK agent region layouts: stack, grid, cluster.
- V1 public SDK agent regions must form one acyclic tree rooted at view_spec.root_region.
- V1 public SDK semantic edges must reference declared substrate.nodes ids.
- V1 public SDK style targets must be explicit refs or unambiguous bare ids.
- V1 public SDK complexity_tier starts at 1, region child bounds are non-negative, and max_children is null or at least min_children.
- V1 public SDK agent style tokens must come from agent-intent-bundle.schema.json, not arbitrary CSS.
- V1 aesthetic profile tokens are deterministic art-direction handles, not CSS: aesthetic.calm_ops, aesthetic.premium_saas, aesthetic.data_dense, aesthetic.editorial_product, aesthetic.executive_review, aesthetic.brutalist, aesthetic.neon_cyber, and aesthetic.warm_organic. The homepage at https://viewspec.dev/ switches between five compiled landing artifacts and exposes https://viewspec.dev/landing-compiled/profile-evidence.json with stable semantic ids, distinct style projection hashes, and 0 shell overrides. At most one profile may target view:{view_spec.id}; profiles may derive governed style projection, bounded layout metadata, and compact style-delta counts in checked manifest summaries, and are not pixel-perfect visual proof, accessibility certification, arbitrary host-app compatibility, or design-review approval.
- The local schema and validate-intent reject hosted-only fields: root design, root motif_library, view_spec.inputs, view_spec.projections, and view_spec.rules.
- Unknown local V1 extension fields fail with UNKNOWN_FIELD instead of being ignored.
- Hosted launch emitters: HTML/Tailwind, React TSX, SwiftUI, Flutter.
- Hosted extended demo artifacts declare `contract_profile: "hosted_extended_v1"` when they include inputs, rules, projections, or mobile emitters beyond the local V1 `validate-intent` contract.
- Free hosted tier: 500 calls/day.
- Pro tier: $149/month, 10,000 hosted compile calls/day, mobile emitters, and 5 custom motif instances per compile.
- Enterprise tier: custom volume, unlimited custom motifs, org sharing, and launch support.
- Public facts manifest: https://viewspec.dev/public-facts.json
- Host proof assertion requirements: `assertion_requirements` reports expected `dom_count`, `style_assertion_count`, and manifest-derived `aesthetic_layout_assertion_count`, `aesthetic_profile_assertion_count`, and `grid_span_assertion_count` before observed grid column/span counts, profiled aesthetic marker/layout assertions, and action payload checks are accepted.
- Proof scope: ViewSpec prove is not pixel-perfect visual regression, accessibility certification, arbitrary host-app certification, or hosted compiler publish automation.

## Short Description

ViewSpec is an agent-native app compiler: agents describe UI and app intent once as IntentBundle or AppBundle JSON, and ViewSpec compiles deterministic UI, state reducers, shell artifacts, and proof reports without asking agents to author DOM or state code.
